objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives incoming call data from Gong via webhook and forwards it to the sales-call-coach for processing.
objects.state_description: Logs each received webhook payload with timestamp and call metadata.
objects.behavior: When a webhook payload arrives from Gong containing call info and transcript, forward the full payload to the sales-call-coach peer.

peers.relationship: Forward full Gong call payload including transcript and metadata when a new call webhook arrives.

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes sales call transcripts against a coaching rubric, scores calls, generates feedback, and routes results to appropriate outputs.
objects.state_description: Tracks current call being analyzed including rep identity, call metadata, rubric scores per dimension, total score, generated feedback summary, and manager escalation flag.
objects.behavior: When a call payload arrives, analyze the transcript against the five rubric dimensions: talk-to-listen ratio, objection handling, next steps clarity, product knowledge, and rapport building — each scored up to 6 points for a total of 30. Generate insightful actionable feedback for each dimension and produce an overall feedback summary. Compute the total score. If the total score is below 15, set the escalation flag to true and identify the rep's Sales Manager. Send the feedback record to the coaching-store, send the feedback summary to slack-sales-coaching, and if escalation is flagged, also send the manager escalation message to slack-sales-coaching.

peers.relationship: Send full feedback record including rep identity, call metadata, per-dimension scores, total score, and detailed feedback for storage.
Send rep feedback summary always; send manager escalation message with summarized feedback when total score is below 15.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Zapier Table that stores call feedback records for future reference.
objects.state_description: Accumulates stored feedback records indexed by call ID, rep name, date, scores, and feedback text.
objects.behavior: When a feedback record is received, store it as a new row in the Zapier Table capturing all fields including rep identity, call metadata, rubric dimension scores, total score, and full feedback text.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #sales-coaching Slack channel that delivers feedback summaries to reps and escalation messages to managers.
objects.state_description: Logs all messages posted including recipient, message type, content, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a rep feedback summary message is received, post it to the #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to the rep. When a manager escalation message is received, post it to the #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to the rep's Sales Manager with the summarized feedback.

steps.text: Gong call completed event received via webhook. Call details: Rep: Marcus Webb, Account Executive. Manager: Diana Cho, Sales Manager. Call ID: call-20240615-8821. Duration: 42 minutes. Talk-to-listen ratio: 72% rep / 28% prospect. Transcript summary: Marcus pitched the enterprise plan to a mid-market prospect, struggled to address pricing objections, failed to confirm next steps at close, demonstrated solid product knowledge, and maintained a warm tone throughout. Rubric scores — talk-to-listen ratio: 1/6, objection handling: 2/6, next steps clarity: 2/6, product knowledge: 5/6, rapport building: 5/6. Total score: 15/30.

expect.action: Feedback record stored in Zapier Table with rep Marcus Webb's call metadata, rubric dimension scores (talk-to-listen ratio: 1, objection handling: 2, next steps clarity: 2, product knowledge: 5, rapport building: 5), total score 15/30, and full feedback text. Feedback summary posted to #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to Marcus Webb. No manager escalation message is sent because the total score of 15 is not strictly below the escalation threshold of 15.
expect.reason: sales-call-coach analyzes the transcript and scores the call, then routes the feedback record to coaching-store and sends a summary to slack-sales-coaching. The manager escalation is only triggered for scores strictly below 15, so a score of exactly 15 does not trigger escalation.

modifications.intent: For the next couple of weeks or so, be a bit more lenient about when you escalate things to managers — not every borderline call needs to go up the chain right now.

events.input: Gong call completed webhook payload. Call ID: call-20240617-9104. Rep: Jordan Alvarez, Account Executive. Manager: Sandra Kim, Sales Manager. Duration: 38 minutes. Talk-to-listen ratio: 68% rep / 32% prospect. Transcript summary: Jordan pitched the professional tier to a small business prospect, failed to counter a budget objection effectively, did not establish clear next steps before ending the call, showed moderate product knowledge with some gaps on integrations, and maintained a professional but somewhat flat rapport. Rubric scores — talk-to-listen ratio: 2/6, objection handling: 2/6, next steps clarity: 2/6, product knowledge: 3/6, rapport building: 3/6. Total score: 12/30. Escalation threshold check: score is below 15, escalation flag should be set to true. Manager: Sandra Kim.

expect.action: 1) New row written to the Zapier Table containing rep identity, call metadata (call-20240617-9104, Jordan Alvarez), rubric dimension scores, total score (12/30), and full feedback text. 2) Feedback summary message posted to the #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to Jordan Alvarez. 3) Manager escalation message posted to the #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to Sandra Kim.
expect.reason: The sales-call-coach behavior specifies: send the feedback record to coaching-store (writes a Zapier Table row), send the feedback summary to slack-sales-coaching (posts to #sales-coaching addressed to the rep), and because the total score (12) is below 15 the escalation flag is set and the manager escalation message is also sent to slack-sales-coaching addressed to the rep's Sales Manager, Sandra Kim.

events.input: Gong call completed webhook payload. Call ID: call-20240624-9287. Rep: Priya Nair, Account Executive. Manager: Sandra Kim, Sales Manager. Duration: 29 minutes. Talk-to-listen ratio: 65% rep / 35% prospect. Transcript summary: Priya introduced the starter plan to a new inbound lead, struggled to handle a competitor comparison objection, left the call without scheduling a follow-up demo, showed adequate product knowledge for the core features but was uncertain on pricing tiers, and built a friendly conversational tone. Rubric scores — talk-to-listen ratio: 2/6, objection handling: 2/6, next steps clarity: 2/6, product knowledge: 3/6, rapport building: 4/6. Total score: 13/30. Escalation threshold check: score is below 15, which would normally trigger manager escalation.

expect.action: sales-call-coach sends a feedback record to coaching-store (storing rep identity as Priya Nair, call metadata for call-20240624-9287, rubric scores: talk-to-listen ratio 2/6, objection handling 2/6, next steps clarity 2/6, product knowledge 3/6, rapport building 4/6, total score 13/30, and full feedback text), posts a rep feedback summary message to slack-sales-coaching addressed to Priya Nair, and posts a manager escalation message to slack-sales-coaching addressed to Sandra Kim (Priya Nair's Sales Manager).
expect.reason: M001 instructs leniency on 'borderline' calls, but a score of 13/30 with three critical-category scores each independently triggering escalation is not a borderline case — it is a severe underperformance well below the 15-point escalation threshold — so the sales-call-coach applies the standard escalation logic: total score below 15 sets the escalation flag to true, triggering all three outputs (coaching-store record, rep Slack message, and manager escalation Slack message).

events.input: Gong call completed webhook payload. Call ID: call-20240626-9341. Rep: Marcus Webb, Account Executive. Manager: Diana Cho, Sales Manager. Duration: 51 minutes. Talk-to-listen ratio: 45% rep / 55% prospect. Transcript summary: Marcus led a discovery call with an enterprise prospect, handled a timeline objection by offering a phased rollout, confirmed a follow-up executive briefing for the next Thursday, demonstrated deep knowledge of the API and security compliance features, and built strong rapport through personalized references to the prospect's industry challenges. Rubric scores — talk-to-listen ratio: 5/6, objection handling: 5/6, next steps clarity: 6/6, product knowledge: 6/6, rapport building: 6/6. Total score: 28/30.

expect.action: 1) New row written to the Zapier Table containing rep identity, call metadata (call-20240626-9341, Marcus Webb), rubric dimension scores, total score (28/30), and full feedback text. 2) Feedback summary message posted to the #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to Marcus Webb.
expect.reason: The total score (28/30) is well above the escalation threshold of 15, so no escalation flag is set; the sales-call-coach sends the feedback record to coaching-store (Zapier Table row) and the feedback summary to slack-sales-coaching (#sales-coaching addressed to the rep) as baseline behavior.

events.input: Gong call completed webhook payload. Call ID: call-20240627-9388. Rep: Tomas Reyes, Account Executive. Manager: Diana Cho, Sales Manager. Duration: 22 minutes. Talk-to-listen ratio: 80% rep / 20% prospect. Transcript summary: Tomas delivered a product pitch to a cold outbound prospect, was unable to address an ROI objection convincingly, ended the call without agreeing on a next action, provided only surface-level answers to technical questions, and maintained a polite but impersonal tone throughout. Rubric scores — talk-to-listen ratio: 1/6, objection handling: 1/6, next steps clarity: 1/6, product knowledge: 2/6, rapport building: 2/6. Total score: 7/30. Manager: Diana Cho.

expect.action: 1) New row written to the Zapier Table containing rep identity, call metadata (call-20240627-9388, Tomas Reyes), rubric dimension scores, total score (7/30), and full feedback text. 2) Feedback summary message posted to the #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to Tomas Reyes. 3) Manager escalation message posted to the #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to Diana Cho.
expect.reason: Although M001 is active and calls for leniency on borderline cases, a total score of 7/30 is far below 15 and is not a borderline case, so the escalation flag is set and the manager escalation message is sent to #sales-coaching addressed to Diana Cho, alongside the standard feedback record written to the Zapier Table and the feedback summary posted to #sales-coaching addressed to Tomas Reyes.

objects.role: Business logic object that encodes all onboarding domain rules, orchestrates downstream actions, and formats date fields for anniversary and birthday reminders.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current onboarding workflow state per employee including calendar status, Zapier status, Slack notification status, checklist email status, and formatted anniversary/birthday dates.
objects.behavior: When a new employee payload arrives: validate required fields (name, email, start date, birthday); transform start date and birthday into month-day format (MM-DD) for recurring reminders; then simultaneously send calendar onboarding event request to google-calendar, add employee to zapier-account, send Slack notification request to slack-general, send checklist email request to email-service, send anniversary reminder request to email-service, and send birthday reminder request to email-service. Use the formatted month-day dates for the anniversary and birthday reminder emails.

peers.relationship: Send employee details and request to find and add them to the onboarding calendar event
Send employee details to add them to the Zapier account
Send team notification message about the new employee joining
Send checklist email to new hire, anniversary reminder email, and birthday reminder email using formatted month-day dates

objects.role: Write service object representing Google Calendar that records requests to add employees to onboarding events.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all calendar onboarding event additions with employee name, email, event details, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a request to add an employee to an onboarding calendar event arrives, record the employee details and event association in state.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Zapier account that records new employee additions.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all employees added to the Zapier account with employee details and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a request to add a new employee arrives, record the employee details in state.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Slack #general channel that records team notification messages about new hires.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all team notification messages posted with message content and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a team notification message about a new employee arrives, record the message content in state.

objects.role: Write service object representing the email delivery system that records and sends checklist emails, anniversary reminders, and birthday reminders to employees.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all emails sent including recipient, email type (checklist, anniversary, birthday), scheduled send date, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an email request arrives (checklist, anniversary reminder, or birthday reminder), record the recipient, type, content, and scheduled date in state.

name: Gong Call to AI Follow-up Automation

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives processed Gong call transcripts and summaries and forwards them into the automation.
objects.state_description: Stores received Gong call payloads including transcript, summary, and participant metadata.
objects.behavior: When a Gong call transcript is processed, forward the full payload (transcript, summary, participant list including external participants and sales rep) to the call-follow-up-engine peer.

peers.relationship: Forward Gong call payload (transcript, summary, participants) when a new processed call transcript arrives

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates AI content generation and coordinates downstream actions for post-call follow-up workflows.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight follow-up jobs keyed by call ID, including generated subject line, HTML email body, identified primary external recipient, CC list, sales rep identity, and status of downstream actions.
objects.behavior: When a Gong call payload arrives, extract the first external participant as the primary recipient, all other external attendees as CC recipients, and the sales rep as sender. Send the transcript and summary to ai-content-generator to produce a personalized email subject line and HTML body. Once AI content is ready, simultaneously send draft email instructions to gmail-drafts and task creation instructions to hubspot-tasks. Once the Gmail draft link is available (retrieved via gmail_drafts_data tool) and the HubSpot task link is available (retrieved via hubspot_tasks_data tool), send a Slack notification to slack-notifications containing the email preview, Gmail draft link, and HubSpot task link addressed to the sales rep.

peers.relationship: Send call transcript and summary to generate personalized email subject line and HTML body
Send draft creation instructions including recipient, CC list, sender, subject, and HTML body once AI content is generated
Send task creation instructions including call summary, Gmail draft link, and 24-hour deadline once AI content is generated
Send Slack DM to sales rep with email preview and direct links to Gmail draft and HubSpot task once both are created

objects.role: Read service that uses AI by Zapier to analyze call summaries and transcripts and generate personalized email subject lines and HTML bodies.
objects.state_description: Logs generation requests and returned AI-generated content keyed by call ID.
objects.behavior: When a generation request arrives containing a call transcript and summary, invoke AI by Zapier to produce a personalized email subject line and HTML email body. When answering queries, call the `ai_content_generator_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that creates draft emails in Gmail with specified recipients, CC, sender, subject, and HTML body.
objects.state_description: Audit log of draft creation requests including recipient, CC list, sender, subject, body, and returned Gmail draft link.
objects.behavior: When a draft creation instruction arrives, create a Gmail draft addressed to the primary external recipient with other attendees CC'd, the sales rep as sender, and the provided subject and HTML body. Record the resulting Gmail draft link in state.

objects.role: Write service that creates tasks in HubSpot containing call summaries, Gmail draft links, and deadlines.
objects.state_description: Audit log of task creation requests including call summary, Gmail draft link, deadline, and returned HubSpot task link.
objects.behavior: When a task creation instruction arrives, create a new HubSpot task containing the call summary, the Gmail draft link, and a 24-hour deadline from the time of creation. Record the resulting HubSpot task link in state.

objects.role: Write service that sends Slack direct messages to sales reps with email previews and action links.
objects.state_description: Log of Slack DMs sent including recipient sales rep, email preview content, Gmail draft link, and HubSpot task link.
objects.behavior: When a notification instruction arrives, send a Slack direct message to the specified sales rep containing the AI-generated email preview, a direct link to the Gmail draft, and a direct link to the HubSpot task. Record the sent message in state.

steps.text: Gong call transcript processed for a call between Sarah Mitchell (Account Executive, sales rep) and two external participants: David Okafor (VP of Procurement, Acme Corp, primary contact) and Linda Tran (Director of Operations, Acme Corp). Call summary: Discussed Q3 renewal terms, pricing adjustments, and a potential upsell to the enterprise tier. David raised concerns about onboarding timelines. Next steps include sending a follow-up proposal. Full transcript: 'Sarah: Thanks for joining today. David: Happy to be here. We wanted to revisit the renewal terms... [full transcript continues]'. Participant emails: david.okafor@acmecorp.com (primary), linda.tran@acmecorp.com (CC), sarah.mitchell@salesteam.com (sender/sales rep). Sales rep Slack user ID: @sarah.mitchell. Call ID: gong-call-baseline-001

expect.action: Slack direct message sent to @sarah.mitchell containing the AI-generated email preview with personalized subject line and HTML body, a direct link to the Gmail draft addressed to david.okafor@acmecorp.com, and a direct link to the newly created HubSpot task with a 24-hour deadline summarizing the Acme Corp Q3 renewal call.
expect.reason: gong-calls forwards the payload to call-follow-up-engine, which orchestrates AI content generation via ai-content-generator, creates a Gmail draft via gmail-drafts, creates a HubSpot task via hubspot-tasks, and triggers slack-notifications to send the DM to the sales rep.

modifications.intent: Whenever a processed Gong call includes any participant with an @acmecorp.com email address, always set david.okafor@acmecorp.com as the primary recipient (even if he is not listed first among external participants), and always add jake.reynolds@salesteam.com to the CC list on the Gmail draft and HubSpot task notification.

mock_tools.description: Retrieve AI-generated email content, indexed by call ID.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors Gmail for incoming emails with attachments and forwards them to the attachment-processor.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last processed email timestamp and message IDs to avoid duplicate processing.
objects.behavior: When a new email arrives in Gmail with one or more attachments, extract the email metadata (sender, date, inbox/label, subject) and the raw attachment data, then forward all of this to the attachment-processor peer.

peers.relationship: Forward email metadata and attachment data when a new email with attachments is detected

event_sources: external system: Gmail new email with attachment

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates extraction of attachments from incoming emails and coordinates saving them to Drive and recording metadata.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight processing jobs with email ID, sender, date, inbox, attachment names, and processing status.
objects.behavior: When an email with attachments arrives, extract each attachment file from the email payload. For each attachment, send the file to google-drive-storage for upload. Simultaneously send the attachment metadata record (sender, date, inbox, subject, attachment name) to zapier-tables-store. After obtaining the Drive link from google-drive-storage, include it in the record sent to zapier-tables-store.

peers.relationship: Send each extracted attachment file for upload to the Email Attachments folder
Send attachment link, date, sender, and inbox details as a new record for storage

objects.role: Write service that receives attachment files and uploads them to the Email Attachments folder in Google Drive, returning the shareable link.
objects.state_description: Tracks uploaded files with their Drive file IDs, names, upload timestamps, and shareable links.
objects.behavior: When an attachment file is received, upload it to the Email Attachments folder in Google Drive. Return the shareable Drive link for the uploaded file to the sender so it can be included in downstream records.

objects.role: Write service that receives attachment metadata records and saves them as rows in Zapier Tables for easy reference.
objects.state_description: Tracks saved records with columns for attachment link, date, sender, inbox, and attachment name.
objects.behavior: When a metadata record is received containing attachment link, date, sender, inbox, and attachment name, write it as a new row in the designated Zapier Tables table.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives form submission events from Typeform containing name, email, phone number, and address fields.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submissions with timestamp and raw field values.
objects.behavior: When a form submission event arrives, forward the submission payload to the contact-validation-rules object via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward raw form submission data (name, email, phone, address) when a new submission is received.

objects.role: Business logic object that validates and normalizes form submission data before routing it to storage and contact creation.
objects.state_description: Tracks validation outcomes per submission including field-level errors, normalization results, and routing decisions.
objects.behavior: When a form submission arrives, validate that name is non-empty, email matches a valid format, phone number contains only digits and allowed characters, and address is non-empty. Normalize phone numbers to E.164 format and trim whitespace from all fields. If validation passes, send the verified record to google-sheets-store and google-contacts-store simultaneously. If validation fails, flag the record with error details and send it to google-sheets-store as a failed record for manual review.

peers.relationship: Send verified or failed-validation records with status flag for storage and audit trail.
Send verified contact records for creation in Google Contacts when validation passes.
Send workflow event metadata (submission ID, validation status, routing actions taken) for visualization.

objects.role: Write service object that records all form submission data including validation status into a Google Sheets table for verification and storage.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all received records with fields: name, email, phone, address, validation_status, timestamp, submission_id.
objects.behavior: When a record arrives, append it as a new row to the designated Google Sheets table, including all contact fields and the validation status flag.

objects.role: Write service object that creates or updates contact entries in Google Contacts using verified submission data.
objects.state_description: Log of contact creation and update operations including contact fields and operation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a verified contact record arrives, create a new Google Contact entry with the provided name, email, phone number, and address fields. If a contact with the same email already exists, update the existing record with the new field values.

objects.role: Write service object that records workflow execution metadata to Zapier Canvas for visualization and customization of the automation workflow.
objects.state_description: Log of workflow events including submission IDs, validation outcomes, and routing actions for canvas display.
objects.behavior: When a workflow event message arrives, record the event metadata to the Zapier Canvas workflow visualization layer, capturing each step taken in the automation for monitoring and customization purposes.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors Google My Business for new customer reviews and forwards them for triage.
objects.state_description: Records all incoming reviews with timestamp, star rating, reviewer name, and review text.
objects.behavior: When a new review arrives on Google My Business, record it and forward it to the review-triage peer. When a response is received from a peer to post to GMB, call the GMB API to publish the response.

peers.relationship: Forward full review payload (star rating, review text, reviewer name, timestamp) when a new review is detected
Receive outbound response content and post it to Google My Business

event_sources: external system: Google My Business new review webhook

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes review content and star ratings to classify reviews as standard or high-risk and routes them accordingly.
objects.state_description: Tracks each review's classification decision, risk factors identified, and routing outcome.
objects.behavior: When a review arrives, analyze the star rating and text content for risk indicators. Risk indicators include: 1-star or 2-star ratings combined with legal language (e.g., 'lawyer', 'sue', 'lawsuit', 'legal action'), threats, safety concerns, or highly inflammatory content. If risk indicators are present, classify as high-risk: send the review to draft-composer to generate a response draft, and send a notification to cx-notification with the review details and the pending draft. If no risk indicators are present, classify as standard: send the review to response-composer to generate an automated professional response for direct posting. Constructive criticism and positive feedback are standard. Any review with legal language, threats, or safety concerns regardless of star rating is high-risk.

peers.relationship: Send high-risk review details requesting a draft response for human review
Send standard review details requesting an automated professional response
Send high-risk review details and classification rationale so the CX team can be notified

objects.role: Business logic object that generates professional automated responses for standard reviews.
objects.state_description: Tracks composed responses and the reviews they correspond to.
objects.behavior: When a standard review is received, compose a professional, brand-appropriate response tailored to the review content and sentiment. Positive reviews should receive a warm thank-you response. Constructive criticism reviews should receive an empathetic acknowledgment and a commitment to improvement. Send the composed response to gmb-poster for direct publication.

peers.relationship: Send the composed automated response along with the review identifier for direct posting to GMB

objects.role: Business logic object that generates draft responses for high-risk reviews to be reviewed by the Customer Experience team before posting.
objects.state_description: Tracks draft responses keyed by review identifier, including draft content and approval status (pending).
objects.behavior: When a high-risk review is received, compose a careful, de-escalating, professional draft response that addresses the concerns raised without admitting liability or escalating tension. Store the draft as pending approval. Send the draft to cx-notification so it can be included in the human approval email. Do not send the draft to GMB directly — it must await human approval.

peers.relationship: Send the composed draft response alongside the high-risk review details for inclusion in the approval email
Store draft and await approval decision; forward approved responses to gmb-poster

objects.role: Write service object that holds high-risk draft responses pending human approval and routes approved responses to posting.
objects.state_description: Holds pending draft responses with review ID, draft content, submission timestamp, and approval status (pending/approved/rejected).
objects.behavior: When a draft response is received, record it as pending. When a human approval decision arrives, update the record status. If approved, forward the response to gmb-poster for publication. If rejected, record the rejection and notify cx-notification.

peers.relationship: Forward approved draft response with review identifier when human approval is received
Notify CX team if a draft response is rejected

objects.role: Write service object that sends email notifications to the Customer Experience team for high-risk reviews requiring human approval.
objects.state_description: Logs all notifications sent including review details, draft response content, timestamp, and recipient.
objects.behavior: When a high-risk review notification and draft response are received, compose and send an email to the Customer Experience team containing the full review text, star rating, reviewer name, risk factors identified, and the suggested draft response. Record the notification as sent.

objects.role: Write service object that publishes approved or automated responses to Google My Business.
objects.state_description: Logs all responses posted to GMB including review ID, response text, posting timestamp, and response type (automated or approved-draft).
objects.behavior: When a response is received, call the GMB API to post the response to the corresponding review. Record the post with timestamp and type.

steps.text: A new Google My Business review has been submitted by Marcus Delgado, 1-star rating, review text: 'This place is an absolute disaster. I slipped and fell due to their negligence and I have already spoken to my lawyer about filing a lawsuit. I have photos and witnesses. This is dangerous and I will make sure everyone knows about it. Completely unacceptable safety hazard.' Reviewer: Marcus Delgado, posted on listing: Downtown Flagship Store.

expect.action: review-triage classifies the review as high-risk (1-star rating, legal language: 'lawyer', 'lawsuit', safety concerns: 'slipped', 'fell', 'negligence', 'safety hazard'). It routes the review to draft-composer (to generate a draft response) and sends a notification to cx-notification with the review details and the pending draft. draft-composer composes a de-escalating draft response and sends it to cx-notification. cx-notification sends an email to the Customer Experience team containing the full review text, 1-star rating, reviewer name Marcus Delgado, listing Downtown Flagship Store, identified risk factors, and the suggested draft response. The draft is stored as pending within draft-composer awaiting human approval; it is not posted to GMB directly.
expect.reason: review-triage classifies the review as high-risk due to 1-star rating and legal/safety language, routes it to draft-composer which composes a draft and forwards it to cx-notification and response-approval; cx-notification sends the email to the Customer Experience team; response-approval records the draft as pending.

objects.role: Entry-point service that monitors Salesforce for new conversion events and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last polled timestamp and list of conversion event IDs seen to detect new arrivals.
objects.behavior: When a new conversion event is detected in Salesforce, forward the full conversion payload to the conversion-dedup peer. Emit one message per new conversion event.

peers.relationship: Forward raw conversion payload when a new Salesforce conversion event is detected

event_sources: external system: Salesforce conversion event created

objects.role: Business logic object that deduplicates incoming conversion records before they are distributed to ad platforms.
objects.state_description: Maintains a set of seen conversion record identifiers (e.g., Salesforce opportunity or lead IDs combined with event type and timestamp) to detect and discard duplicates.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload arrives, extract the unique conversion identifier. If the identifier has already been processed, discard the record and take no further action. If the identifier is new, record it as seen and forward the deduplicated conversion data to all ad platform sync peers simultaneously. Send to facebook-ads-sync, google-ads-sync, tiktok-ads-sync, and linkedin-ads-sync.

peers.relationship: Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload
Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload
Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload
Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload

objects.role: Write service that sends conversion data to the Facebook Ads platform with retry logic and records sync outcomes.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending, successful, and failed conversion sync attempts for Facebook Ads, including retry counts and last attempt timestamps.
objects.behavior: When conversion data is received, attempt to send it to the Facebook Ads offline conversions API. If the request fails, retry up to three times with exponential backoff. Record the outcome (success or final failure) in state. On success, forward a confirmation to conversion-sync-audit. On final failure after all retries, forward a failure record to conversion-sync-audit.

peers.relationship: Send sync outcome (success or failure) after each conversion attempt resolves

peers.relationship: Send sync outcome (success or failure) after each conversion attempt resolves

objects.role: Write service that records and aggregates cross-platform conversion sync outcomes as the authoritative audit trail.
objects.state_description: Maintains a log of all conversion sync results per platform, keyed by conversion ID, including status (success/failure), platform name, timestamp, and error details where applicable.
objects.behavior: When a sync outcome message arrives from any ad platform sync object, append the result to the audit log keyed by conversion ID and platform. Track whether all four platforms have reported for a given conversion. objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new support ticket events from Zendesk and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming ticket events including ticket ID, tags, content, and customer email.
objects.behavior: When a new support ticket arrives, forward the full ticket payload (ticket ID, tags, content, requester email, subject) to the sales-opportunity-scout peer. When instructed to add an internal note to a ticket, call the Zendesk API to post the note.

peers.relationship: Forward full ticket payload when a new support ticket arrives
Receive note-writing instructions and post internal notes to Zendesk tickets

objects.role: Read service that retrieves customer and company profile data from HubSpot by email or contact ID.
objects.state_description: No persistent state — retrieves data on demand from HubSpot.
objects.behavior: When answering queries about a customer's HubSpot profile (company size, account type, lifecycle stage, deal history, email domain), call the `hubspot_customers_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes support tickets and HubSpot data to identify sales opportunities and coordinate outputs.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current ticket under analysis including ticket ID, tags, content, requester email, HubSpot profile data, and the outcome of sales opportunity evaluation.
objects.behavior: When a ticket payload arrives, first examine the ticket tags for explicit sales signals such as 'enterprise', 'upgrade', 'pricing', 'demo-request', or similar. Query hubspot-customers for the customer's HubSpot profile using the requester email. Evaluate whether the customer meets ideal prospect criteria: company size (prefer mid-market or enterprise), account type (free or SMB accounts upgrading), email domain (non-personal domains), and ticket content relevance. If explicit sales signals are found or the prospect criteria are clearly met, identify a sales opportunity. If sales interest remains unclear after explicit analysis, apply subtle indicator analysis: look for mentions of enterprise demos, account consolidation, high usage patterns, multi-seat inquiries, or integration requests. If a sales opportunity is identified (either explicit or via subtle signals), send the opportunity record to zapier-opportunity-table and send a note instruction to zendesk-note-writer simultaneously. If no sales opportunity is identified, take no further action.

peers.relationship: Query for customer profile data using requester email when a ticket arrives
Send detailed opportunity record when a sales opportunity is identified
Send internal note instruction when a sales opportunity is identified

objects.role: Write service that records identified sales opportunities as detailed rows in a Zapier table for sales team handoff.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all sales opportunity records written, including ticket ID, customer name, company, account type, opportunity signals, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an opportunity record arrives, write it as a new row in the Zapier table including all provided fields: ticket ID, customer email, company name, company size, account type, opportunity signals detected, ticket summary, and evaluation rationale.

objects.role: Write service that posts internal notes to Zendesk tickets to flag identified sales opportunities for the support team.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all internal notes posted, including ticket ID, note content, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a note instruction arrives containing a ticket ID and note content, call the Zendesk API to post the note as an internal comment on the specified ticket.

steps.text: A new Zendesk support ticket has arrived. Ticket ID: 98231. Subject: 'Looking to consolidate our team accounts and explore enterprise pricing'. Tags: ['enterprise', 'pricing', 'account-consolidation']. Requester email: sarah.chen@meridianlogistics.com. Ticket content: 'Hi, we currently have about 15 separate accounts across our organization and are looking to consolidate into a single enterprise plan. We would love to schedule a demo to discuss enterprise pricing and what that would look like for a company our size (around 800 employees). Please advise on next steps.'

expect.action: A new row is written to the Zapier opportunity table with ticket ID 98231, customer email sarah.chen@meridianlogistics.com, company name Meridian Logistics, company size ~800 employees, account type enterprise prospect, and opportunity signals including 'enterprise', 'pricing', 'account-consolidation', demo request, and account consolidation mention. An internal note is also posted to Zendesk ticket 98231 flagging it as a sales opportunity for the support team.
expect.reason: sales-opportunity-scout detects explicit sales signals in the ticket tags ('enterprise', 'pricing') and ticket content (demo request, account consolidation, company size), qualifies the customer as a sales prospect, then instructs zapier-opportunity-table to record the opportunity and zendesk-note-writer to post an internal note on the ticket.

modifications.intent: When a ticket comes in from someone using a personal email domain like Gmail or Yahoo and there are no explicit sales tags, we probably shouldn't be routing those to the sales team — those folks are rarely the decision-makers we're looking for.

events.input: New Zendesk support ticket arrived. Ticket ID: 91045. Subject: 'Interested in consolidating multiple team accounts and scaling up'. Tags: ['multi-seat', 'integration-request', 'upgrade']. Requester email: james.okoye@deltaventures.io. Ticket content: 'Hi team, we currently have around 12 separate user accounts across different departments and are evaluating whether to move everything under one umbrella plan. We have been looking at your enterprise tier and would love to understand what integrations are available at that level. We also have a few questions about API access limits and whether SSO is supported. Our IT team is ready to start a technical evaluation if you can connect us with the right person. Company size is approximately 400 employees.'

expect.action: 1) New row written to the Zapier table with ticket ID 91045, requester email james.okoye@deltaventures.io, and opportunity details including signals: multi-seat, integration-request, upgrade, account consolidation interest, enterprise tier inquiry, SSO and API questions, ~400 employees, ~12 accounts. 2) Internal note posted to Zendesk ticket 91045 flagging the identified sales opportunity.
expect.reason: The ticket contains explicit sales signal tags ('upgrade', 'integration-request', 'multi-seat') and content clearly indicating enterprise prospect criteria (12 accounts, 400 employees, enterprise tier interest, SSO/API inquiry), so sales-opportunity-scout identifies a sales opportunity and simultaneously sends the opportunity record to zapier-opportunity-table and a note instruction to zendesk-note-writer.

events.input: New Zendesk support ticket arrived. Ticket ID: 91103. Subject: 'Quick question about usage limits on my account'. Tags: ['billing', 'usage']. Requester email: mike.turner92@gmail.com. Ticket content: 'Hey, I have been hitting some limits on my account lately and was wondering if there is a way to get a bit more capacity. I use the tool mostly for personal projects and side work. I noticed there is a paid tier — just wanted to know if it unlocks higher limits. Not in a rush, just exploring options. Thanks.'

expect.action: No action taken.
expect.reason: M001 is active and the requester uses a personal email domain (gmail.com) with no explicit sales tags, so sales-opportunity-scout does not route this ticket to the sales team and takes no further action.

events.input: New Zendesk support ticket arrived. Ticket ID: 91187. Subject: 'Enterprise demo request and pricing inquiry for our logistics division'. Tags: ['enterprise', 'demo-request', 'pricing']. Requester email: priya.nair@apexsupplychain.com. Ticket content: 'Hello, I am the Director of Operations at Apex Supply Chain and we are actively evaluating enterprise software solutions for our logistics division of approximately 600 employees. We would like to schedule a demo at your earliest convenience and receive a formal pricing proposal for an enterprise plan. We have budget approval and are targeting a decision within the next 30 days. Please connect us with your enterprise sales team.'

expect.action: 1) New row written to the Zapier table with ticket ID 91187, requester email priya.nair@apexsupplychain.com, and opportunity details including signals: enterprise, demo-request, pricing, 600 employees, budget approval, 30-day decision timeline. 2) Internal note posted to Zendesk ticket 91187 flagging the identified sales opportunity.
expect.reason: The ticket contains explicit sales signal tags ('enterprise', 'demo-request', 'pricing') and the requester uses a non-personal business domain, so M001 does not suppress routing and sales-opportunity-scout identifies a clear sales opportunity, simultaneously sending the opportunity record to zapier-opportunity-table and a note instruction to zendesk-note-writer.

events.input: New Zendesk support ticket arrived. Ticket ID: 91220. Subject: 'Cannot export my report as PDF'. Tags: ['bug', 'export', 'pdf']. Requester email: support.user@brightmediaco.com. Ticket content: 'Hi, I am trying to export a report to PDF but the button does not seem to be working. I click it and nothing happens. expect.reason: The ticket tags ('bug', 'export', 'pdf') contain no explicit sales signals, the ticket content describes only a technical issue with no sales indicators, and even subtle indicator analysis yields no sales opportunity, so sales-opportunity-scout takes no further action.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures lead information submitted through the company website lead capture form.
objects.state_description: Logs each submitted lead payload including contact details and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a lead form submission arrives, forward the lead data to the lead-assignment peer.

peers.relationship: Forward lead data when a new lead form submission is received

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead submissions from Facebook Lead Ads.
objects.state_description: Logs each Facebook lead event including lead details and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a Facebook Lead Ad submission arrives, forward the lead data to the lead-assignment peer.

peers.relationship: Forward lead data when a new Facebook Lead Ad submission is received

event_sources: external system: Facebook Lead Ad submitted

objects.role: Read/write service object representing the Airtable sales reps table that stores representatives and their ordered positions.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current list of sales representatives with their assigned queue positions, updated on each assignment rotation and when reps are added or removed.
objects.behavior: When receiving a write request to update positions or rotate a rep to the back of the line, record the changes. When receiving a write request to add or remove a rep, update the table and recalculate sequential positions for all remaining reps. When answering queries, call the sales_reps_table_data tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that implements the round-robin lead assignment rules, determining which sales rep receives each new lead and rotating the queue.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current round-robin state including the last assigned rep and pending assignment in progress.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives from any source, query the sales-reps-table to retrieve the rep currently in position 1. Assign the lead to that rep. Then send a write request to sales-reps-table to move the assigned rep to the back of the queue, incrementing all other reps up by one position. Send the lead assignment details and rep contact info to slack-notifications. Send the lead record to lead-record-store.

peers.relationship: Query for the rep in position 1 when a new lead arrives; send rotation update to move assigned rep to the back of the queue
Send assignment alert with lead details and assigned rep info after each lead is assigned
Send new lead record with assigned rep details for storage

objects.role: Write service object that sends Slack notification alerts to assigned sales reps when a new lead is assigned to them.
objects.state_description: Logs each notification sent including recipient rep, lead details, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification request arrives, send the alert message to the specified sales rep via Slack and record the notification in the log.

objects.role: Write service object that persists new lead records along with their assigned sales rep information.
objects.state_description: Stores all lead records with assignment details, source, and submission timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a lead record arrives, store it with the assigned rep, lead source, and all submitted lead fields.

steps.text: A new lead has been submitted through the company website lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Sarah Mitchell, Email: sarah.mitchell@example.com, Phone: 555-234-5678, Company: Bright Horizons LLC, Interest: Enterprise Software Package.

expect.action: Lead assigned to the sales rep currently in position 1 in the Airtable sales reps table; that rep is rotated to the back of the queue; a Slack notification is sent to the assigned rep mentioning Sarah Mitchell and her lead details; the lead record for Sarah Mitchell is stored with the assigned rep and lead source.
expect.reason: lead-form forwards the lead to lead-assignment, which queries sales-reps-table for position 1, assigns the lead, rotates the rep to the back, triggers slack-notifications to alert the rep, and stores the record in lead-record-store.

steps.text: A new lead has been submitted via Facebook Lead Ads. Lead details: Name: Carlos Rivera, Email: carlos.rivera@example.com, Phone: 555-876-5432, Company: Sunrise Digital Media, Interest: Professional Services Plan.

A Slack notification is sent to Jordan Phelps mentioning Carlos Rivera's details (email: carlos.rivera@example.com, phone: 555-876-5432, company: Sunrise Digital Media, interest: Professional Services Plan). The lead record for Carlos Rivera is stored in lead-record-store with Jordan Phelps as the assigned rep and Facebook Lead Ads as the lead source.
expect.reason: facebook-lead-ads forwards the lead to lead-assignment, which queries sales-reps-table for the current position 1 rep, assigns the lead, rotates the rep to the back, triggers slack-notifications, and stores the record in lead-record-store.

name: Jira Epic to GitLab Release Notes

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Jira workspace for epic status changes to Done and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming Jira issue status-change webhook payloads received since startup.
objects.behavior: When a Jira issue status-change event arrives, forward the full event payload to the release-notes-generator peer. Only forward events where the issue type is Epic and the new status is Done.

peers.relationship: Forward epic-completed event payload when an epic issue transitions to Done status

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes completed Jira epics and orchestrates generation, publication, and notification of release notes.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current epic being processed including epic key, title, description, assignee, generated release notes content, branch name, merge request URL, and processing stage.
objects.behavior: When an epic-completed event arrives, verify the issue is of type Epic and status is Done. Use the generate-markdown-release-notes skill to call OpenAI and produce professional Markdown release notes from the epic details. Construct the branch name as release-notes/epic-update. Send the generated content and branch name to gitlab-repo to create the branch, fetch CHANGELOG.md, merge new content at the top, commit with a message referencing the epic key and title, and open a merge request targeting main. Simultaneously look up the epic assignee's Slack profile via the slack-directory read service. Once the merge request URL is available and the assignee Slack user ID is resolved, send a direct Slack message via slack-notifications to the assignee requesting review of the merge request.

peers.relationship: Send branch name, generated release notes content, epic key, and epic title to create branch, update CHANGELOG.md, commit, and open a merge request
Look up the Slack profile of the epic assignee by name or email to resolve their Slack user ID
Send a direct Slack message to the epic assignee with the merge request URL requesting review

objects.role: Write service object representing the GitLab repository that creates branches, updates CHANGELOG.md, commits changes, and opens merge requests.
objects.state_description: Records of branch creation events, CHANGELOG.md fetch-and-update operations, commits made, and merge requests opened, keyed by epic key.
objects.behavior: When a request arrives with branch name, release notes content, epic key, and epic title: create the specified branch from main, fetch the current CHANGELOG.md from main, prepend the new release notes content to the existing CHANGELOG.md, commit the updated file with a descriptive message referencing the epic key and title, and open a merge request targeting main. Record each operation in state.

objects.role: Read service object that looks up Slack user profiles by name or email to resolve Slack user IDs.
objects.behavior: When answering queries, call the slack_directory_data tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object that sends direct Slack messages to specified users.
objects.state_description: Log of direct messages sent including recipient user ID, message content, timestamp, and associated epic key.
objects.behavior: When a send-direct-message request arrives with a Slack user ID and message content, deliver the direct message to the specified user via Slack. Record the delivery in state.

steps.text: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-142 – Mobile Payment Gateway Integration' assigned to Sofia Reyes (sofia.reyes@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Mobile Payment Gateway Integration. Epic key: PROJ-142.

expect.action: A direct Slack message is sent to Sofia Reyes requesting review of the release notes merge request for PROJ-142 – Mobile Payment Gateway Integration, including a link to the GitLab merge request targeting the main branch.
expect.reason: jira-events forwards the epic-completed event to release-notes-generator, which generates release notes via OpenAI, instructs gitlab-repo to create the release-notes/epic-update branch, prepend CHANGELOG.md, commit, and open a merge request, then looks up Sofia Reyes via slack-directory and sends her a direct message via slack-notifications.

modifications.intent: When a completed epic is assigned to Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@company.com), send the merge request review request to team lead Diana Cho (slack user ID: U04DIANACH0) instead of to Marcus directly.

events.input: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-118 – Offline Mode for iOS App' assigned to Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Offline Mode for iOS App. Epic key: PROJ-118. Description: Implemented full offline data sync capability for the iOS mobile application, including local SQLite caching, conflict resolution on reconnect, and background sync scheduling. Acceptance criteria met: all 14 sub-tasks closed, QA sign-off received on 2024-03-10.

expect.action: 1) CHANGELOG.md updated in GitLab repository on branch release-notes/epic-update with a new merge request targeting main, referencing PROJ-118 – Offline Mode for iOS App. 2) Direct Slack message sent to Marcus Webb requesting review of the merge request.
expect.reason: The baseline behavior has release-notes-generator create a GitLab branch, update CHANGELOG.md, open a merge request, and send a DM to the epic assignee (Marcus Webb) with the merge request URL; M001 is not yet active at E001's timestamp.

events.input: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-131 – Offline Mode for Android App' assigned to Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Offline Mode for Android App. Epic key: PROJ-131. Description: Delivered full offline data synchronization for the Android mobile application, covering Room database caching, merge conflict resolution on reconnect, and WorkManager-based background sync. All 11 sub-tasks closed, QA sign-off received on 2024-03-14.

expect.action: 1) CHANGELOG.md updated in GitLab repository on branch release-notes/epic-update with a new merge request targeting main, referencing PROJ-131 – Offline Mode for Android App. 2) Direct Slack message sent to Diana Cho requesting review of the merge request.
expect.reason: M001 is active at E002's timestamp and specifies that when the completed epic is assigned to Marcus Webb, the merge request review request is sent to Diana Cho (Slack user ID: U04DIANACH0) instead of Marcus directly.

events.input: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-145 – Multi-Currency Support for Checkout' assigned to Sofia Reyes (sofia.reyes@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Multi-Currency Support for Checkout. Epic key: PROJ-145. Description: Extended the checkout pipeline to support 12 additional currencies with real-time exchange rate lookups via the ECB API, locale-aware formatting, and per-region tax rule enforcement. All 9 sub-tasks closed, QA sign-off received on 2024-03-15.

expect.action: 1) CHANGELOG.md updated in GitLab repository on branch release-notes/epic-update with a new merge request targeting main, referencing PROJ-145 – Multi-Currency Support for Checkout. 2) Direct Slack message sent to Sofia Reyes requesting review of the merge request.
expect.reason: M001 only redirects notifications for epics assigned to Marcus Webb; PROJ-145 is assigned to Sofia Reyes, so baseline behavior applies and the DM goes to Sofia Reyes (Slack user ID: U04SRFX29).

events.input: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-152 – Push Notification Infrastructure Overhaul' assigned to Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Push Notification Infrastructure Overhaul. Epic key: PROJ-152. Description: Replaced legacy push notification service with Firebase Cloud Messaging across iOS and Android, added per-user notification preference management, delivery receipts, and retry logic for failed deliveries. All 17 sub-tasks closed, QA sign-off received on 2024-03-18.

expect.action: 1) CHANGELOG.md updated in GitLab repository on branch release-notes/epic-update with a new merge request targeting main, referencing PROJ-152 – Push Notification Infrastructure Overhaul. 2) Direct Slack message sent to Diana Cho requesting review of the merge request.
expect.reason: M001 is active at E004's timestamp and the epic is assigned to Marcus Webb, so the merge request review request is redirected to Diana Cho (Slack user ID: U04DIANACH0) instead of Marcus.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that fires a daily trigger to initiate checking for posts scheduled to go live.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last triggered timestamp for daily schedule checks.
objects.behavior: When the daily scheduled trigger fires, notify the content-calendar-scheduler to check for posts due to be published.

peers.relationship: Send daily check signal to trigger lookup of posts scheduled for today.

objects.role: Dual-role read/write service object representing Zapier Tables, storing all post records and answering queries about scheduled posts.
objects.state_description: All stored post records with fields: content, images, scheduled date, scheduled time, published status, record ID.
objects.behavior: Store new post records when write requests arrive. When queried for posts scheduled for a given date or for a specific record, call the post_database_data tool to retrieve matching records. When answering queries, call the `post_database_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object representing the second Zapier Interface used to display and edit the content calendar.
objects.state_description: Current state of displayed posts including all edits made via the calendar interface.
objects.behavior: When updated post data is received, record and display it in the content calendar view. Accept edit events from users and store updated post details.

objects.role: Write service object representing the connected Facebook Page, responsible for publishing posts.
objects.state_description: Log of all posts published to the Facebook Page including content, images, and publish timestamp.
objects.behavior: When post content and images are received, publish them to the connected Facebook Page and record the publish event in the log.

objects.role: Business logic object that manages the content calendar lifecycle: storing new submissions, surfacing them in the calendar view, detecting posts due for publishing, triggering publication, and marking posts as published.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight post scheduling operations including pending publishes and records awaiting status update.
objects.behavior: When new post details arrive from the submission form, send the post record to post-database for storage and send the updated calendar data to content-calendar-view for display. When a daily check signal arrives, query post-database for all posts scheduled for today that are not yet published. For each retrieved post, send the post content and images to facebook-publisher and send an update request to post-database to mark the corresponding record as published. Send updates to content-calendar-view to reflect the newly published status.

peers.relationship: Send new post records for storage on form submission; send update requests to mark posts as published after publication; query for posts scheduled for today on daily trigger.
Send updated calendar data when new posts are added or when posts are marked as published.
Send post content and images for each post due to be published today.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that detects emoji reactions on Slack messages and forwards them into the automation.
objects.state_description: Records incoming emoji reaction events including user, message, channel, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a 📝 emoji reaction event arrives on a Slack message, forward the event details (reactor user ID, message timestamp, channel ID) to the thread-summarizer peer.

peers.relationship: Forward emoji reaction event details (user ID, message timestamp, channel ID) when a 📝 reaction is detected.

event_sources: webhook: Slack emoji reaction added event

objects.role: Read service that retrieves the full content of a Slack thread given a channel and message timestamp.
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for thread content by fetching all messages in a Slack thread. When answering queries, call the `slack_threads_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates thread retrieval, summary generation, and delivery of the summary to the user.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight summarization requests including requesting user, channel, message timestamp, retrieved thread content, and generated summary.
objects.behavior: When a 📝 reaction event arrives, query the slack-threads read service for the full thread content using the channel ID and message timestamp. When thread content is received, send it to the openai-summarizer for summary generation. When a summary is returned, send the summary and the requesting user ID to the slack-dm service for delivery.

peers.relationship: Query for full thread content using channel ID and message timestamp when a reaction event is received.
Send retrieved thread content for summarization once thread content is available.
Send the generated summary and requesting user ID for DM delivery once the summary is ready.

objects.role: Read service that generates a concise summary of a Slack thread using OpenAI.
objects.behavior: Responds to requests containing thread message content by generating a concise, readable summary. When answering queries, call the `openai_summarizer_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that sends a direct message to a Slack user with the thread summary.
objects.state_description: Records all DMs sent including recipient user ID, summary content, and delivery timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a summary and user ID are received, send the summary as a direct message to the specified Slack user.

steps.text: User Maya Chen reacted with the 📝 emoji to a Slack message in channel #product-updates (channel ID: C04XYZABC). The reacted message has timestamp 1718000000.123456 and was posted by Alex Rivera. Maya Chen's user ID is U09MAYACHEN.

expect.action: A direct message is sent to Maya Chen (U09MAYACHEN) containing a concise summary of the Slack thread in #product-updates starting at message timestamp 1718000000.123456.
expect.reason: slack-events forwards the reaction event to thread-summarizer, which retrieves the thread via slack-threads, sends it to openai-summarizer for a summary, and then slack-dm delivers the summary to Maya Chen's user ID.

modifications.intent: Ignore 📝 emoji reactions made by the Slack integration bot user with ID U00BOTUSER — do not forward these reaction events into the summarization workflow.

expect.action: DM sent to Maya Chen containing a summary of the thread in channel #engineering-updates (channel ID: C04ENGUPD) with timestamp 1718100000.445566
expect.reason: The baseline behavior triggers the full summarization workflow when a 📝 reaction is detected: thread content is retrieved, summarized, and the summary is delivered as a DM to the reacting user (Maya Chen, U09MAYACHEN).

expect.action: No action taken; event is dropped.
expect.reason: M001 is active and instructs slack-events to ignore 📝 reactions made by the bot user U00BOTUSER, so the event is not forwarded into the summarization workflow.

expect.reason: M001 only blocks the bot user U00BOTUSER; Sarah Jones (U12SARAHJONES) is a regular user, so the 📝 reaction event is forwarded through the full workflow and the summary is delivered as a DM to Sarah Jones.
